in Strait of Hormuz;  Mojtaba Khamenei says navy ready to inflict 'bitter defeats' on enemies\n\nTrump says Iran 'cannot blackmail' US \nUS President Donald Trump said that Iran will not be allowed to \"blackmail us\" as the two countries work to end their conflict.\nSpeaking to reporters at the Oval Office, he said that the US was talking to Iran, adding that \"very good conversations\" were going on with Tehran.\n\nPope Leo downplays feud with Trump, says 'not in my interest' to debate him \nPope Leo sought to downplay his feud with US President Donald Trump on Saturday, saying reporting about comments he has made so far during his Africa tour \"has not been accurate in all its aspects.\"\nSpeaking to journalists in English aboard his flight to Angola for the third leg of his ambitious 10-day Africa tour, the first US pope said comments he made two days earlier in Cameroon decrying that the world was being \"ravaged by a handful of tyrants\" were not aimed at Trump.\nThat speech, said Leo, \"was prepared two weeks ago, well before the president ever commented on myself and on the message of peace that I am promoting.\"\n\nRussia's state nuclear energy firm says ready to help with removal of enriched uranium from Iran \nThe head of Russia's state atomic energy company, Rosatom, Alexei Likhachev, said on Saturday that Rosatom is ready to help with the removal of enriched uranium from Iran, and that the company is closely following the progress of US-Iran talks.\n\nNo US-Iran peace talks scheduled in Islamabad on Monday: govt sources \nThe much-anticipated US-Iran peace talks are not taking place in Islamabad on Monday, according to sources.\nTop government sources in Islamabad said that no date for the talks has been decided yet.\n\n\nLebanon president vows to prosecute those who attacked French UN peacekeepers \nLebanese President Joseph Aoun vowed on Saturday to prosecute those who targeted French UN peacekeepers, killing one and wounding three.\nIn a statement shared by the presidency, Aoun expressed his condolences to his French counterpart Emmanuel Macron in a phone call, condemning the incident and vowing that Beirut \"will not hesitate to pursue those involved and bring them to justice\".\n\nHezbollah official says Lebanon-Israel talks ‘do not concern us’ \nSenior Hezbollah official Mahmud Qamati said on Saturday that his group was not concerned by Lebanon’s planned direct talks with Israel, labelling them a failure.\nIn a press conference in Beirut’s southern suburbs, Qamati said his group was \"not concerned with the negotiations being conducted by the state\", saying they were \"a failure, weak, defeated... and submissive negotiations\".\n\"The resistance is the one that imposes. We are the land... and we are the ones who draw up the decisions, not those who have an official status,\" he said, adding that while his group did not mind Beirut \"coordinating with us... not in
